content.problemStatement: Write a C program that swaps the values of two integer variables, a =5 and b = 10, and prints their new values using a single printf statement with a space between them
content.outputFormat: Two space-separated integers on a single line representing the swapped values of a and b.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program swaps the initial values a=5 and b=10 and prints the swapped values.
No input is needed; the output is always the same swapped pair.
Repeated to emphasize that the result does not depend on any external data.

solution.approach: The problem does not require any input. Initialise two integer variables a = 5 and b = 10. Swap their values using a temporary variable (or tuple assignment). Finally, print the swapped values using a single output statement with a space between them. The algorithm runs in constant time and uses constant extra space.
